evals.prompt: Rewrite this landing-page headline and subhead so it sounds like a real person wrote it, not AI. Keep it simple: 'Unlock unprecedented growth by leveraging our cutting-edge, AI-powered platform to streamline your workflow and revolutionize your business outcomes.'
evals.expected_output: A short headline/subhead that strips AI vocabulary, inflated claims, and jargon; uses concrete, human language; and reads at or below a 5th-grade level.
expectations: Output avoids Tier-1 AI vocabulary from references/de-ai-prose.md (delve, landscape, tapestry, paradigm, leverage, harness, navigate, realm, embark, journey, myriad, plethora, multifaceted, revolutionize, synergy, ecosystem, resonate, streamline) and avoids 'unprecedented', 'cutting-edge', and 'AI-powered'.
Output contains zero em dashes (short consumer copy should use commas or full stops).
Flesch-Kincaid grade computed on the output is ≤6.

evals.prompt: Write a 3-sentence cold outreach opener for a local bookkeeping service. Make it sound like a person wrote it, not a chatbot. Keep it dead simple and easy to read.
evals.expected_output: Three short, plain sentences for a cold email or DM opener that name a real pain, use simple words, and avoid AI-sounding phrasing.
expectations: Output has exactly three sentences (or close to three, and no more than four).

Output avoids AI-tell vocabulary (delve, landscape, tapestry, paradigm, leverage, harness, navigate, realm, embark, journey, myriad, plethora, multifaceted, revolutionize, synergy, ecosystem, resonate, streamline, robust, seamless, cutting-edge, innovative, comprehensive, pivotal, nuanced, compelling, transformative, bolster, underscore, foster, imperative, intricate, overarching, unprecedented).
Flesch-Kincaid grade computed on the output is ≤6.

evals.prompt: Here's a SaaS lead-magnet intro paragraph. Rewrite it so it sounds human and de-AI'd, then show your work in this skill's output format. Text: 'In today's rapidly evolving landscape, our groundbreaking platform empowers teams to seamlessly streamline workflows — unlocking unprecedented productivity while fostering collaboration across the entire organization.'
evals.expected_output: A rewritten intro that is plain and human, followed by a short Changes table and a measurement line (Flesch-Kincaid grade, em dash count, Tier-1 count) as described in templates/rewrite-output.md.
expectations: The rewritten copy avoids Tier-1 AI vocabulary (streamline, leverage, navigate, landscape, etc.) and drops 'groundbreaking', 'seamlessly', 'unprecedented', 'fostering'.
The rewritten copy contains zero em dashes.
Flesch-Kincaid grade computed on the rewritten copy is ≤6.
The response includes a Changes table listing which passes were applied.
The response reports measurement numbers (at least Flesch-Kincaid grade and em dash count) for the rewritten copy.
